skill.name: create-skill
skill.description: Guides users through creating or updating AI skills with clear metadata, reusable resources, testing steps, and packaging guidance.
skill.summary: Create focused AI skills with practical structure, examples, and review steps.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are mostly Markdown examples, tool names, paths, and template text rather than executable code. The remaining real risk is moderate: the skill intentionally helps create or update AI skill files and discusses broad tool access, so users should review generated skills before enabling them. No prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or hidden execution was found.

medium_findings.title: Broad Tooling Guidance Can Enable File and Shell Access
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE with legitimate purpose. The skill documents Write, Bash, and unrestricted allowed-tools guidance for skills, which can expand what generated skills may do. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The lines explicitly discuss shell and file-capable tools, but they are guidance text rather than executable code or a hidden command path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence directly describes shell and file-capable tools. Risk depends on how users configure generated skills, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Skill Creation May Write Project or Personal Skill Files
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE with legitimate purpose. The skill guides creation of skill directories, including a personal hidden path under ~/.claude, so it can lead to persistent changes in AI behavior. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The file paths and creation workflow are explicit, but the instructions are transparent and user-facing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly describes creating skill directories and storing personal skills. This is expected behavior for the skill, not evidence of covert filesystem abuse.

low_findings.title: Static Command Detections Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for hidden execution. The detected Bash and script references appear inside Markdown examples or tool descriptions, not executable bundled scripts. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: The cited lines are fenced examples, directory diagrams, or documentation of available tools with no code path that automatically runs commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command-like text is visible instructional content. No executable script file or automatic command invocation was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography alerts point to prose, frontmatter, or examples and no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic implementation is present. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: The cited lines contain ordinary words and template text, not crypto APIs or algorithm choices.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context contains no cryptographic code. The matches are semantic collisions in Markdown text.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Benign Writing Guidance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The scanner hit ordinary guidance about avoiding duplication, asking questions, and designing resources; no host enumeration or data collection intent was found. Confidence: 0.90. Confidence reasoning: The cited lines are workflow guidance for authoring skills, not commands or probes against the local system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is contextual documentation. No reconnaissance command or collection workflow is present in the files reviewed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Broad Tooling Guidance Can Enable File and Shell Access
dangerous_patterns.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE with legitimate purpose. The skill documents Write, Bash, and unrestricted allowed-tools guidance for skills, which can expand what generated skills may do. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The lines explicitly discuss shell and file-capable tools, but they are guidance text rather than executable code or a hidden command path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence directly describes shell and file-capable tools. Risk depends on how users configure generated skills, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

content.value_statement: Creating reusable AI skills is difficult when structure, metadata, resources, and tests are unclear. This skill provides a practical workflow for designing, writing, testing, and packaging focused skills.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md structure and frontmatter fields.
Guides users through defining concrete use cases before writing a skill.
Shows when to add scripts, references, and assets to a skill package.
Provides a reusable Markdown template for new skill instructions.
Includes testing, iteration, and packaging guidance for distributing skills.
Compares skills with slash commands for workflow design decisions.
limitations: It does not validate a completed skill with an automated security scanner.
It does not provide executable build or packaging scripts.
It cannot guarantee that generated skills are safe or effective without review.
It focuses on Claude-style skills and may need adaptation for other platforms.

use_cases.title: Create a New Workflow Skill
use_cases.description: Design a focused skill for a repeated task, including metadata, instructions, and reusable resources.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Review a current skill, reduce duplicated guidance, and move detailed material into references when useful.

use_cases.title: Package Team Knowledge
use_cases.description: Turn team procedures, schemas, templates, or policies into a structured skill that an assistant can use.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new skill outline.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new skill for [task]. Ask me for any missing use cases, then draft the SKILL.md structure.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Reusable Resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request to decide what files belong in a skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these example requests for [domain]. Recommend what should go in SKILL.md, scripts, references, and assets.

prompt_templates.title: Revise an Existing Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request to improve an existing skill package.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill for focus, progressive disclosure, duplicate guidance, and missing testing steps. Propose specific edits.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Distribution
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request before sharing a skill with others.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this skill for required metadata, clear file references, lean instructions, resource organization, and packaging readiness.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for rotating and merging PDF files.
output_examples.output: A focused skill plan with a clear trigger description, a short instruction flow, and a recommendation to include reliable PDF scripts.
A revision plan that keeps core workflow steps in SKILL.md and moves table details into a reference document.

output_examples.input: Package our support escalation process as a skill.
output_examples.output: A proposed skill structure with frontmatter, procedural steps, policy references, and concrete test scenarios.

best_practices: Start from concrete user requests before deciding the skill structure.
Keep SKILL.md focused and move long reference material into separate files.
Test the skill on real tasks before sharing it with other users.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad skills that cover many unrelated workflows.
Do not put large reference documents directly into SKILL.md.
Do not enable broad tool access in generated skills without reviewing the risk.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create or update AI skills with metadata, instructions, reusable resources, tests, and packaging guidance.

faq.question: Does it include a template?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Markdown template that shows common sections for SKILL.md and bundled resources.

faq.question: Can it create skills with scripts?
faq.answer: It explains when scripts may be useful, but users must review any script before execution or distribution.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: The reviewed files show no malicious intent, but generated skills should be reviewed because they may change assistant behavior.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: It is useful for AI workflow builders, maintainers, and teams packaging repeatable procedures for assistants.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: The guidance is written for Claude-style skills, but many design practices also apply to Claude Code and Codex workflows.
